account_id.description: Cloudinary account ID. Falls back to the CLOUDINARY_ACCOUNT_ID environment variable.

account_url.description: Cloudinary account URL in the format account://API_KEY:API_SECRET@ACCOUNT_ID. When set, takes precedence over account_id, api_key, and api_secret. Falls back to the CLOUDINARY_ACCOUNT_URL environment variable.

api_key.description: Cloudinary Provisioning API key. Falls back to the CLOUDINARY_API_KEY environment variable.

api_secret.description: Cloudinary Provisioning API secret. Falls back to the CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET environment variable.

block.description: The Cloudinary Provisioning provider manages account-level Cloudinary resources such as product environments, access keys, and custom policies.

api_secret.description: The API secret. Only available immediately after creation. Preserved in state.

created_at.description: The time at which the access key was created (RFC3339).

enabled.description: Whether the access key is enabled.

name.description: A display name for the access key.

product_environment_id.description: The ID of the product environment this access key belongs to. Changing this value forces a new resource to be created.

updated_at.description: The time at which the access key was last updated (RFC3339).

block.description: Manages an API access key for a Cloudinary product environment. Access keys provide the credentials needed to interact with a product environment's API.

created_at.description: The Unix timestamp at which the custom policy was created.

description.description: A human-readable description of the custom policy.

enabled.description: Whether the custom policy is enabled.

id.description: The unique identifier of the custom policy assigned by Cloudinary.

name.description: The display name of the custom policy.

scope_id.description: The ID of the product environment this policy applies to. Required when scope_type is "prodenv". Must be omitted or empty when scope_type is "account".

scope_type.description: The scope of the policy. Must be one of: "account" (applies to the whole account) or "prodenv" (applies to a specific product environment).

updated_at.description: The Unix timestamp at which the custom policy was last updated.

block.description: Manages a Cloudinary custom permissions policy. Custom policies allow fine-grained access control over Cloudinary resources using Cedar policy language.

principal_id.description: The unique identifier of the principal.

principal_type.description: The type of principal. Must be one of: "user", "group", "apiKey", "provisioningKey".

role_id.description: The unique identifier of the role to assign.

scope_id.description: The ID of the product environment. Required when scope_type is "prodenv". Must be omitted when scope_type is "account".

scope_type.description: The scope level. Must be one of: "account" (whole account) or "prodenv" (specific product environment).

block.description: Assigns a role to a principal (user, group, API key, or provisioning key) within a given scope. All attributes are immutable: any change forces resource replacement.

base_sub_account_id.description: The ID of an existing product environment to copy settings from when creating this one. Changing this value forces a new resource to be created.

cloud_name.description: The Cloudinary cloud name for this product environment. Auto-generated if not provided.

created_at.description: The time at which the product environment was created (RFC3339).

deletion_protection.description: Whether to prevent accidental deletion of this product environment. Defaults to true. When true, terraform destroy will fail with an error. To destroy the resource, set this to false and run terraform apply first.

enabled.description: Whether the product environment is enabled.

id.description: The unique identifier of the product environment assigned by Cloudinary.

name.description: The display name of the product environment.

updated_at.description: The time at which the product environment was last updated (RFC3339).

block.description: Manages a Cloudinary product environment (sub-account). Product environments allow you to segment your Cloudinary usage for different projects or teams.

account_id.description: The Cloudinary account ID the provider is configured for.

principal_id.description: The unique identifier of the current principal.

principal_type.description: The type of the current principal. Currently always "provisioningKey" when authenticating with a provisioning API key.

block.description: Returns the identity of the currently authenticated principal (the credentials used to configure the provider). Use principal_id and principal_type directly in cloudinaryprovisioning_principal_role_assignment.

cloud_name.description: The Cloudinary cloud name for this product environment. Used to look up the environment when id is not set.

created_at.description: The time at which the product environment was created (RFC3339).

enabled.description: Whether the product environment is enabled.

id.description: The unique identifier of the product environment. When set, takes precedence over cloud_name.

name.description: The display name of the product environment.

updated_at.description: The time at which the product environment was last updated (RFC3339).

block.description: Reads information about an existing Cloudinary product environment (sub-account). Look up by id or by cloud_name.

description.description: A short description of the role.

id.description: The unique identifier of the role.

management_type.description: Whether the role is managed by Cloudinary ("system") or by the user ("custom").

name.description: The name of the role to look up (e.g. "Admin").

permission_type.description: The permission type of the role. Must be one of: "global" (role applies across all contexts within its scope, e.g. full account or all folders in a product environment) or "content" (role applies to specific content instances such as a particular folder or collection).

scope_type.description: Where the role is applied: "account" or "prodenv". When provided, only roles matching this scope type are considered, which is useful when multiple roles share the same name across scopes (e.g. "Admin").

block.description: Looks up a Cloudinary role by name and permission type. Use this data source to retrieve the role ID needed for principal role assignments.
